era_context: Early Bronze Age III; Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty IV–V transition); Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia; Mature Indus Valley Civilization. The same broad historical context continues.
geographic_coverage_gaps: East Asia outside the Yellow River valley

events.title: Menkaure's reign — Old Kingdom Egypt
events.description: Menkaure's reign continues. The Old Kingdom state remains stable and centralized. The Pyramid of Menkaure is likely nearing completion or recently completed, representing the final major pyramid construction of Dynasty IV. After Menkaure's reign, pyramid construction will decline in scale, marking a shift in royal priorities and possibly reflecting changes in state resources or ideology.
events.certainty_note: Menkaure's reign is attested, but the specific year -2941 is assigned by chronological convention.

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization — Mature phase
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continues in its mature phase. Urban centers, trade networks, and agricultural production remain stable. No major disruptions are archaeologically evident.
events.certainty_note: The mature Indus Valley Civilization is archaeologically well-attested for this period.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year continues the pattern of sparse documentation for routine years. The historical record for this period is heavily dependent on king-lists and archaeology, with major events (royal deaths, military campaigns) recorded but routine governance largely invisible.
